comment: Published BBN inputs for the TFPT frozen-eta lithium watchdog; retrieval date 2026-07-03. The TFPT baryon density is NOT in this file -- it comes from the frozen records (v84 Omega_b + flat-budget h) and lives in the CLI.

bbn_reference.source: PRIMAT: Pitrou, Coc, Uzan, Vangioni 2018, Phys. Rep. 754, 1 (arXiv:1801.08023); reference point at Planck 2018 omega_b
bbn_reference.scaling_note: local power-law scalings around eta10 ~ 6: Li7/H ~ eta^+2.12, D/H ~ eta^-1.6, Yp ~ eta^+0.04 (Fields 2011, Ann. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 61, 47; adequate over the <2% omega_b range used here)

li7_spite_plateau.source: Sbordone et al. 2010, A&A 522, A26 (metal-poor halo stars, Spite plateau)
li7_spite_plateau.note: A(Li) = 2.199 +- 0.086; the plateau value is stable over 3 dex in metallicity -- the core of the lithium problem

dh_cooke.source: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, ApJ 855, 102 (precision QSO absorption systems)

yp_aver.source: Aver et al. 2021 (He abundance in metal-poor HII regions)

resolution_landscape: proposed resolutions: (a) stellar depletion/atomic diffusion + turbulent mixing in halo stars (supported by lower Li in more metal-poor / cooler stars, NGC 6397 trends); (b) nuclear (ruled out: the 7Be destruction channels are measured); (c) exotic BBN physics (decaying particles, varying constants) -- requires new light states or dials; (d) eta inhomogeneities. TFPT admits only (a): eta is frozen and there is no compiler slot for (c).
